heroDescription: A personal portfolio designed and developed as a product in its own right. It combines an expressive editorial identity with a reusable React case-study system, responsive interactions and a direct route from first impression to meaningful project evidence.

overview.description: Portfolio '25 is the digital home for my work as a Product and UX/UI Designer. The site had to communicate personality quickly, then step back and let each case study demonstrate how I frame problems, make decisions and carry a visual system into implementation.

projectGoal.description: Build a distinctive, performant and maintainable portfolio that helps design leads understand my positioning, scan relevant work and move into detailed evidence without losing the energy of the opening experience.

fontAndColours.description: Gilda Display introduces an editorial, human voice while Geist handles navigation, systems and long-form clarity. Warm orange acts as a signature rather than decoration, punctuating a restrained palette of ink, warm grey and muted slate.

meta.label: Scope
meta.value: Brand, UX, UI, motion and implementation

challenge.description: A portfolio has two audiences and very little time. Hiring teams need evidence they can scan; the designer needs enough expression to be remembered. The challenge was to make the interface feel authored without letting typography, motion or personal storytelling obscure the work.
challenge.question: How might a portfolio feel unmistakably personal in seconds and still reward a careful, evidence-led review?

priorities.title: Position clearly
priorities.description: State what I design, how I think and where craft meets product logic before asking visitors to explore.

priorities.title: Make work scannable
priorities.description: Use consistent project cards and case-study chapters so depth never becomes disorientation.

priorities.title: Build for change
priorities.description: Keep content data-driven and components reusable so the portfolio can evolve with the work.

research.description: The research combined portfolio benchmarking with a content audit of my own projects. The most effective references paired one memorable idea with disciplined navigation; weaker examples either felt interchangeable or forced visual experimentation into every interaction.

benchmark.note: The benchmark evaluates communication patterns and review friction, helping define a portfolio direction rather than rank individual designers.

persona.name: Marta Ríos
persona.context: Marta reviews many portfolios between meetings. The first pass is fast and comparative; deeper reading happens only when role, craft level and project relevance are immediately legible.
persona.quote: Show me the judgement behind the polish. I want to understand why each design decision matters.
goals: Identify relevant product and interface work
Find decisions, constraints and outcomes inside a case study
frustrations: Beautiful intros that delay access to the work
Case studies with no repeatable hierarchy
Contact details hidden behind an over-designed interaction

insights.label: 01 · Memory
insights.title: One visual tension is enough
insights.description: The serif/sans contrast creates a recognisable voice; the rest of the system can remain disciplined.

insights.label: 02 · Evidence
insights.title: Consistency makes depth usable
insights.description: A shared chapter rhythm lets readers compare projects without flattening their individual stories.

insights.label: 03 · Momentum
insights.title: Every page needs a next move
insights.description: Work, next project and contact routes should remain obvious even inside long-form content.

ideation.description: The concept was organised around a simple review loop: understand, scan, inspect and connect. Rather than inventing a different navigation model for each project, expression lives in typography, imagery and transitions while the information architecture remains predictable.

flow.title: Recognise
flow.description: Read the positioning and register the visual identity.

flow.title: Scan
flow.description: Compare four projects through consistent cards and tags.

flow.title: Inspect
flow.description: Enter a structured case study with clear chapter landmarks.

flow.title: Continue
flow.description: Move directly to the next relevant project without returning home.

flow.title: Connect
flow.description: Reach contact and professional profiles from any meaningful endpoint.

decisions.label: Content architecture
decisions.title: Four curated projects
decisions.description: A smaller, stronger set allows each case to receive depth and reduces the burden of comparison.

decisions.label: Page system
decisions.title: Data over duplication
decisions.description: One shared React template turns project JSON into a consistent long-form experience.

decisions.label: Identity
decisions.title: Editorial meets systematic
decisions.description: Gilda carries personality and Geist carries interface logic, mirroring the balance in the work.

decisions.label: Motion
decisions.title: Transitions carry continuity
decisions.description: Curtains, restrained reveals and the next-project interaction connect pages without slowing reading.

design.description: The interface uses scale, contrast and generous negative space to create an editorial rhythm. Motion is applied at entry points and media reveals, while long-form content relies on a stable two-column grid and modular evidence blocks.

decisions.label: Typography
decisions.title: Two voices, distinct jobs
decisions.description: Gilda communicates authorship; Geist provides precision across labels, navigation and case-study content.

decisions.label: Colour
decisions.title: Orange signs the work
decisions.description: The accent marks focus and identity while warm neutrals keep large pages comfortable to read.

decisions.label: Grid
decisions.title: Asymmetry with anchors
decisions.description: Large headings and offset imagery feel expressive because dates, dividers and content columns stay aligned.

decisions.label: Performance
decisions.title: Motion respects the page
decisions.description: Transforms and image curtains avoid layout shifts, and reduced-motion preferences keep the experience usable.

applications.objective: Communicate role, personality and craft before visitors choose a project.
applications.solution: A concise positioning statement, visual signature and immediate project transition share the opening screen.

applications.objective: Show thinking and outcomes with enough depth while keeping every project structurally consistent.
applications.solution: Ten reusable chapters connect context, research, decisions, validation, final design and learning.
applications.outcome: The portfolio shows judgement as clearly as output.

applications.objective: Preserve editorial pacing and project evidence on narrow viewports.
applications.solution: Responsive type, stacked evidence and in-flow media preserve the same narrative at narrow widths.
applications.outcome: The mobile version feels composed rather than reduced.

testing.description: Validation treated the portfolio as a review tool. Tasks focused on whether a first-time visitor could identify my role, choose a relevant project, understand a design decision and reach contact details across desktop and mobile.
testing.method: Scenario-based reviews combined content comprehension, keyboard/navigation checks and responsive browser walkthroughs. Findings describe prototype and implementation decisions, not recruitment outcomes.

findings.task: Describe the designer after the opening view
findings.observation: The visual identity was memorable, but role language needed to be as direct as the typography was expressive.
findings.decision: Pair the creative headline with a concise Product Designer statement and concrete areas of practice.

findings.observation: Project names alone did not reveal enough difference during a fast scan.
findings.decision: Add consistent discipline, platform and context tags to every card.

findings.task: Find evidence of process on mobile
findings.observation: A wide process layout made readers hunt through one continuous text column.
findings.decision: Use numbered chapters, compact visual modules and clear section dividers at every breakpoint.

finalDesign.description: The final portfolio is intentionally dual in character: expressive at first contact, systematic once the work begins. Project data, reusable modules and a shared responsive grid allow every case to feel complete without rebuilding the page around it.

highlights.title: Authored introduction
highlights.description: An oversized typographic identity establishes personality while preserving immediate access to featured work.

highlights.title: Curated work index
highlights.description: Four projects use common tags and image behaviour to support quick, meaningful comparison.

highlights.title: Reusable case studies
highlights.description: A single component system renders deep project narratives from maintainable, project-specific data.

highlights.title: Continuous contact path
highlights.description: Next-project and contact moments create a natural route through the work without navigation dead ends.

impact.description: The portfolio functions as both a personal brand and a scalable publishing system. Its impact is expressed through the design infrastructure delivered: four coherent cases, one maintainable template and a responsive language shared across the experience.

metrics.label: Curated body of work
metrics.description: Each project carries its own context and visual identity inside a consistent review structure.

metrics.label: Expressive hierarchy
metrics.description: Editorial expression and interface clarity work as a deliberate, reusable identity system.

metrics.label: Shared publishing system
metrics.description: Content can evolve without duplicating page components or fragmenting responsive behaviour.

learnings.description: Designing for yourself still requires product discipline. The strongest personal expression came from choosing a few repeatable rules, then letting the quality and context of the work provide the variation.

items.title: A portfolio is an interface for evidence
items.description: Visual identity earns attention, but hierarchy and consistency determine whether the work can be understood.

items.title: Constraints make personality clearer
items.description: A defined type pairing, accent and motion grammar produce more recognition than constant visual reinvention.

items.title: Content architecture is part of the design
items.description: A reusable case-study model improved not only maintenance, but the quality of the story each project could tell.

gallery.description: Desktop and mobile mockups showing the editorial home experience, project-page continuity, next-project transition and responsive featured-work system.
